TextENGNew: "...Towada-san.@k@vS10/40/410300828. Could you... move aside from there?"
Towada-san, sitting in front of the closet doors, flinched for an instant. ...But she quickly put on a pleasant expression and tilted her head.
changeReason: Old says singular 'closet door'; new says plural 'closet doors' (押入れの扉). Old says 'began trembling'; new says 'flinched' (びくっと), which is more accurate as a sudden startle vs ongoing trembling.

TextENGNew: "Sorimachi-san... is something the matter?"
"You're... also...@k@vS10/40/410300830. hiding something... aren't you?"
The phrasing was technically a question, but there was no expectation of an answer. ...It was closer to a confirmation.
changeReason: Old adds 'nonchalantly' and names 'Miyuki-chan' (neither in JPN); new translates more literally. Old says 'she wanted to confirm something she already knew' (adds 'she already knew'); new says 'It was closer to a confirmation' (matching JPN 確認に近かった).

TextENGNew: Once again, I found myself unable to understand what kind of person Miyuki-chan really was.
That easygoing tone, that lovely smile...
changeReason: Old uses full sentence 'She spoke in a calm tone'; new uses noun phrase 'That easygoing tone, that lovely smile' matching the JPN fragment style (おっとりとした口調、可憐な笑顔). Also 'calm' vs 'easygoing' for おっとり (gentle/easygoing) is a nuance shift.

TextENGNew: But the words she occasionally spoke carried a viscous, venomous quality — like a snake coiling around its prey and squeezing tight.
changeReason: New includes 'viscous' (粘着質な) and 'occasionally' (時折), which old omits. Old simplifies 底意地の悪さ (deep-seated malice) to just 'venom'; new preserves the sticky, clinging quality described in JPN.

TextENGNew: And her eyes were shining like the blade of a finely sharpened knife.
"It's not a big deal.@k@vS10/40/410300832. But would you... step aside and let me check over there?"
"Ah... sure..."
Her tone sounded intimidating. ...It was hard to believe that voice came from someone with such a gentle face.
And Towada-san quickly rose to her feet under the pressure.
However... even though she stood up, she didn't move from that spot.
"Miyuki-chan... what's wrong?"
"You'll understand once you see.@k@vS10/40/410300834. Something's in the closet.@k@vS10/40/410300835. ...Well, Towada-san?"
"...?@k@vS10/42/410600333. Wh-what is it...?@k@vS10/42/410600334. What do you think's in there...?"
"You'll know once you see it.@k@vS10/40/410300837. So please, step aside."
"Sorimachi-san... what grounds do you have for saying that...?"
"Don't change the subject.@k@vS10/40/410300839. ...Or what, do you want me to say everything?"
Towada-san did her best to resist Miyuki-chan's frightening glare... but that sharp gaze was too much for her to bear, so she couldn't find the words to respond.
But even so, she maintained her position in front of the closet and refused to move aside.
"...Can you tell us?@k@vS10/40/410300841. If you're willing to talk, I won't pry any further... How about it?"
"J-just what do you think you're talking about?!"
The two of them fiercely glared at one another from point-blank range.
And then... Miyuki-chan let out a brief sigh, shook her head, and casually adjusted her hair as she continued.

"...Okay.@k@vS10/40/410300844. Then, it can't be helped.@k@vS10/40/410300845. I'll explain it myself.@k@vS10/40/410300846. ...What happened to your lover, Kurosawa-kun...?@k@vS10/40/410300847. You know where he is, don't you?"
"W-why would I?!@k@vS10/42/410600340. And how does that have anything to do with this closet?!"
"...I saw it earlier.@k@vS10/43/410400548. There was a human hand inside that closet... right?"
The moment Sonozaki-san chimed in, Towada-san gasped.
And... Miyuki-chan's facial expression didn't change at all, like she already knew.
Hey... wait a minute?
A human hand...? In that closet?
You can't be serious!
Why would something like that be in this closet?!
"Wh-what do you mean...?"
"Seriously... what's this about a hand?@k@vS10/62/410101472. You're saying something like that is here?! Hey!!"
I rose to my feet, pushed Towada-san aside, and put a hand on the sliding door.
...I could see her gasping face out of the corner of my eye, but that didn't matter now.
A hand...? It can't be... There's no way a person could be hiding inside there...!
*rattle*
Several comforters were neatly stacked on the upper shelf. And multiple futons were carefully folded on the bottom shelf.
I looked for this human hand... but couldn't see it anywhere.
"...What is this?@k@vS10/62/410101475. I don't see a hand anywhere!"
I felt relieved.
Indeed. There's no way a human hand would be in a place like that, right?
What were the two of them talking about?
"Mion-san, what is that supposed to mean...?@k@vS10/42/410600346. You saw a hand...?"
"I wonder...?@k@vS10/43/410400552. I'm just telling you what I saw.@k@vS10/43/410400553. Besides... I haven't said a word to implicate you as the culprit."
"I guess you were just mistaken about what you saw?@k@vS10/42/410600348. If there was a human hand in a place like this, I would've noticed it when I pulled out a blanket earlier."
"...Is that so?"
Miyuki-chan stared back at Towada-san. Her eyes were bitterly cold.
"Ryuunosuke-san, would you give me just a moment?"
Miyuki-chan pushed me aside and stood directly in front of the closet. Then, she carefully pulled the comforters down, one by one.
"Wait... Please, stop!@k@vS10/42/410600351. If you insist on pulling out the futons, I'll do it!"
"That's ok, I don't want to pull out the futons."
"E-enough already...!@k@vS10/42/410600353. What's with you all of a sudden...?!"
As Miyuki-chan pushed Towada-san aside, she reached into the bedding... and hit something.
Then, she turned to look at the girl for a moment... before pulling out the comforter.
The next moment...
Hanging there...
Something fell out from the space between the futons.
And... it was... a pale human hand...?!
"Hyaah?!"
"Wh... what is this?!"
Miyuki-chan grabbed the hand and pulled without a second thought.
Given its position, it required an incredible amount of force to pull "it" out.
Before long... I heard the owner of that hand tumbling down to the floor.
"N-no way...?!"
His face... it was Kurosawa, the man who was sleeping here just a little while ago.
"Kya... kyaaaaaaaahh!!!"
Towada-san turned and screamed. She covered her mouth with both her hands and stared on with a look of bewilderment in her eyes.
"...W-why? How?!@k@vS10/42/410600356. What is Takumi-kun doing in a place like this?!@k@vS10/42/410600357. Takumi-kun! Takumi-kun?!"
Within a moment, Towada-san was clinging to Kurosawa.
...But he didn't respond to her call... His head and hands drooped with no strength behind them... and he didn't move at all.
"I-it can't be...?!@k@vS10/42/410600359. Takumi-kun! Takumi-kun... w-why?!@k@vS10/42/410600360. Aaah... aaaaaaaaaahh!!!"
Towada-san continued clinging to Kurosawa as she cried out.
But his face was pale and he didn't move a muscle.
...He was clearly dead.
"Towada-san...@k@vS10/62/410101479. How did this...?"
"No way... I can't believe someone's really dead..."
"Uu...!@k@vS10/42/410600364. Takumi-kun, Takumi-kun...! Aaahh...!!"
...However.
Two sets of eyes were staring intently at Towada-san.
Sonozaki-san... and Miyuki-chan.
"Miyuki-chan...?"
"...Are you done now?"
"H-hold on, Miyuki-chan...@k@vS10/62/410101482. Her boyfriend just died. She's grieving. Can't you choose your words more carefully?"
"That's... rather cold of you."
Otobe watched on and spoke in an admonishing tone. However, Miyuki-chan didn't respond to either of us beyond a distorted smile on the edge of her lips.
"Well, call me coldhearted if you will."
"Towada-san... I need you to let me investigate this corpse for a moment."
Without giving Towada-san an opportunity to refuse, Miyuki-chan put her hands on Kurosawa's corpse and started checking his pupils.
She was like a professional handling an autopsy.
Sonozaki-san approached the body too, and began examining his face.
And then, their eyes slowly turned to Towada-san.
"...Anyway, are you done now?@k@vS10/43/410400555. I do have a question... You knew about this from the start, didn't you?"
"You knew that Takumi Kurosawa's corpse was in here the whole time."
"Huuh?!"
"W-whaa?!"
Otobe and I both raised our voices in surprise.
Then, Towada-san emphatically gasped and opened her eyes wide in shock.
"Well?@k@vS10/43/410400558. Tell us the truth."
"That's not... That's not true!@k@vS10/42/410600369. Takumi-kun disappeared, and I... I...!"
"...Stop hiding things. Didn't we have this discussion earlier?"
"...Please, stop joking around!!@k@vS10/42/410600371. Don't chastise me and say whatever you please!!"
Towada-san shouted emotionally and stared with determination.
However... her gaze quickly fell to the floor, as if repelled.
"...You knew, didn't you?@k@vS10/43/410400563. Isn't that why you didn't want us to look?"
Before long.
She realized it would be meaningless to say anything in her defense... so Towada-san faintly nodded with a fatigued expression on her face.
"Why were you hiding this until now?@k@vS10/43/410400565. You should have consulted with us."
"I'm sorry...@k@vS10/42/410600376. The truth is... I... found the corpse a little while ago.@k@vS10/42/410600377. In here, when I opened the closet..."
"So... you knew Kurosawa was dead in there?!@k@vS10/62/410101485. For how long?!"

"Around when the men in black were making a ruckus and all of you were outside.@k@vS10/42/410600379. ...I wasn't feeling well, so I went to the closet hoping to get myself a pillow...@k@vS10/42/410600380. But... Takumi-kun... Takumi-kun's corpse was... Uuu...!"
"Then, you're saying Kurosawa was already in there by the time we came back here?!"
"I'm sorry...@k@vS10/42/410600382. But... I was afraid you'd suspect me... so I couldn't say anything."
"Suspect you...?@k@vS10/62/410101488. Why would we suspect you?"
"Because... aside from me... there was nobody else here...@k@vS10/42/410600384. If you found him... I'd be the first to be suspected...!"
Towada-san's voice was hoarse and quivering intermittently.
I understand how she must've felt, but... doesn't hiding it makes it even more suspicious?
"...You were completely overthinking it.@k@vS10/62/410101490. If you had told us about this from the beginning, we would've stood by your side."
"I'm so sorry...@k@vS10/42/410600386. When I saw Takumi-kun's body, I couldn't understand it. I panicked...@k@vS10/42/410600387. I was alone and didn't know what to do..."
"...Haah."
For now... though I was still hesitant, that was enough to explain Towada-san's suspicious behavior. But regarding what happened afterwards, and who killed Kurosawa...
"Ah, but..."
Just then.
Otobe seemed to have regained his composure, and spoke up while rubbing his neck.
"What is it, Otobe?"
"Well... about that.@k@vS10/41/410200758. ...Doesn't that sound a little strange...?"
"What about it is strange?"
"Because... Kurosawa-san was attacked near the shrine gate, wasn't he...?@k@vS10/41/410200760. So why would the culprit bring him to a place like this...?"

"Now that you mention it, that's true.@k@vS10/62/410101495. I was near the entrance of the assembly hall during that entire gunfight.@k@vS10/62/410101496. There's no way that someone could have snuck a corpse in here without being seen, right...?"
Thinking it over... a terrible thought crept up on me.
It was an absolutely impossible situation for a murder.
"Right... that's right.@k@vS10/62/410101498. When did the killer transport the corpse here... without us noticing?"

"I don't know...@k@vS10/42/410600389. Maybe it was right after the attack near the shrine gate... when everyone had gone to the ritual storehouse, or when you were out searching for Takumi-kun... Maybe that's when the killer brought him here...?"
"Then even back then... Kurosawa-san was already dead?!"
"Hey, wait.@k@vS10/62/410101500. Isn't that a little odd?@k@vS10/62/410101501. Miyuki-chan, Otobe, and I very abruptly decided to tag along to go the ritual storehouse, didn't we?"
"What would the culprit have done if one of us decided to stay here instead of going to the ritual storehouse?!"
"I can't imagine what the killer was thinking..."
Towada-san muttered that, then hung her head. ...Looking at that, we all started to calm our emotional outburst.
...I can imagine what she's feeling.
Finding a corpse right in front of us and setting aside our feelings of sympathy in order to start deducing the circumstances of the murder. Is that really human?
Towada-san approached Kurosawa's corpse and put both her hands on his face.
"I should have... brought you out sooner.@k@vS10/42/410600392. ...Sorry, Takumi-kun..."
Though Kurosawa's face was pale, he looked like he was simply asleep and feeling unwell.
But... the reality was far more cruel.
"Takumi-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600394. I was afraid of being suspected... so I did something terrible...@k@vS10/42/410600395. I did something terrible...@k@vS10/42/410600396. I'm sorry, Takumi-kun..."
Right after she said that, tears came spilling out of Towada-san once again.
...But the next moment.
"...It looks like there aren't any visible wounds.@k@vS10/43/410400567. ...So, what killed Takumi Kurosawa?"
"It's a simple question.@k@vS10/43/410400569. ...Isn't it natural to wonder?"
"Sonozaki-san, now may not be the best time to say something like that..."
"But it's strange, so I'm telling you it's strange."
Sonozaki-san paid no heed to my attempts to keep her in check and continued making her point.
"How was Takumi Kurosawa killed?@k@vS10/43/410400572. If he was stabbed, there would've been bleeding. And if he was pushed around near the stone steps, there would've been mud on his body, right?"
"And yet, this corpse is perfectly clean.@k@vS10/43/410400574. Why is that?"
"...Yeah, now that you mention it."
That certainly seemed to be the case. ...When I looked at his feet, I couldn't see even a trace of mud on his socks or the hem of his pants.
"There are no visible marks on this man.@k@vS10/43/410400576. So, what is the cause of death?"
"Why... How can that be...?"
Towada-san looked back and forth between Otobe and me.
All Otobe and I could do was tilt our heads in bewilderment.
Checking dead bodies was absolutely out of the question for me.
I had no problem with living humans, but as soon as they die they become a frightening object.
Thinking about it that way, I was somewhat alarmed by Miyuki-chan's lack of aversion to looking over the corpse.
Investigating corpses is not the kind of job for average people.
...Before long, Miyuki-chan seemed to finish her investigation. She looked back and spoke in an emotionless clerical tone.
"The cause of death is strangulation.@k@vS10/40/410300861. There are cord markings near his cervical vertebrae."
Cord markings near his cervical vertebrae...?
Cervical vertebrae... meaning his neck? And... what are these cord markings?

"Cord markings are impressions left behind when a thin rope is tightened around a body.@k@vS10/40/410300863. These marks are typically found on the skin of the neck when someone is killed by strangulation."
Miyuki-chan could tell from our faces that we didn't understand, so she offered a detailed explanation.
"...Unfortunately, he couldn't have been killed while we were dealing with the men in black.@k@vS10/40/410300865. And he didn't die while we were in the ritual storehouse either."
"Judging by the state of rigor mortis, Kurosawa-kun died around 9 PM last night."
"Around... 9 PM...?"
I thought desperately about what Miyuki-chan said as I repeated her words back to her... until finally, I realized.
"H-hold on.@k@vS10/62/410101508. Isn't that before we even arrived here?"
"...Indeed."
"But that's weird!@k@vS10/41/410200765. Because we all saw him."
"Right, Arakawa-san? Isn't that so?@k@vS10/41/410200767. Kurosawa-san was sleeping here drunk, wasn't he?!@k@vS10/41/410200768. So..."
"Aah?!"
"Huh?!"
Otobe was so alarmed by my hysterical exclamation that he turned and looked me straight in the eyes.
"What is it...?"
"Maybe at that time... Kurosawa was already..."
A horrifying thought came rushing out.
Kurosawa, who was supposedly sleeping in the corner of the room after heavy drinking, was in fact already dead...?!
If that's the case... then there's only one possibility.
"Towada-san... you..."
"I... I don't know!@k@vS10/42/410600399. I don't know anything!@k@vS10/42/410600400. Seriously, I didn't know!@k@vS10/42/410600401. ...?!"
"...Enough already."
Miyuki-chan grabbed Towada-san by the hand... and looked her straight in the eyes.
There was no longer any hint of a smile on her face.
Her outrage was so horrifyingly powerful... that even I was trembling a short distance away.
"...I'll ask you once more.@k@vS10/40/410300870. Kurosawa-san was killed before we even arrived here...@k@vS10/40/410300871. right?"
Towada-san's whole body was trembling... but even so, she continued forcefully shaking her head to deny it.
"I don't know...@k@vS10/42/410600404. Really... I don't know...!"
"It wasn't me! I didn't do it!@k@vS10/42/410600406. Please, believe me!!"
Perhaps driven to her limit by that display, Miyuki-chan turned on her heels and walked out of the room.
And, before I could even ask where she was going, she came back.
In her hands, wrapped in a handkerchief... she had a rope... roughly one meter long.
"Th-that's...!!"
"...I found this in the kitchen trash can earlier.@k@vS10/40/410300874. It was very well hidden, so it was really just a coincidence that I found it."

"If we give this to the police, I'm sure they'll find traces of the skin from Kurosawa-kun's neck.@k@vS10/40/410300876. And... tightening the rope by hand was a very poor decision.@k@vS10/40/410300877. After all, the ends of the rope are sticky with sweat..."
As soon as Towada-san heard all that... her eyes opened wide and she lost her voice.
...Miyuki-chan wasn't simply doubting her. She knew all this from the beginning.
And there was no point examining the rope any further... Towada-san's reaction made everything abundantly clear.
Dejectedly,
Towada-san's eyes were open wide as she stared at the rope. ...And eventually, she let out a dry laugh. Then, she leaned her back against the wall while muttering to herself.
"...Aaah, I give up.@k@vS10/42/410600415. Who would've thought people like detectives would be in a place like this?"
"...Even though I went through so much effort to choose a location where nobody would ever go..."
"Towada-san...?!"
"...Just a little longer... and I could've regained my peaceful life.@k@vS10/42/410600418. What a shame..."
...Towada-san looked down at Kurosawa's corpse with cold eyes as she dispassionately spoke.
She was crying over Kurosawa just a moment ago, but her cold-hearted tone made it clear that was just an act.
Just as Miyuki-chan and Sonozaki-san had realized, that crying earlier was an utter lie.
"It's just as Sorimachi-san says.@k@vS10/42/410600420. I killed Takumi-kun before all of you arrived here..."
"Towada-san...?@k@vS10/41/410200772. So... when you told us you and Kurosawa-san were attacked near the shrine gate... that was a lie, too...?"
"Of course."
Towada-san deeply nodded.
Towada-san... really is a murderer?!
Towada-san suddenly felt like a creature from another world.
Towada-san looked back at me and smiled, as though she'd perceived my thoughts.
Perhaps it was an act of strength from a person who'd been completely driven to the wall with all avenues of escape cut off.
But... the horrifying smile on her face... showed no signs of life.
